name: pytest-unique

metadata.name: pytest-unique
metadata.summary: Pytest fixture to generate unique values.

documentation:
# pytest-unique

[Pytest](http://pytest.org) fixture to generate unique values.

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue.svg
:alt: License
:target: https://github.com/cr3/pytest-unique/blob/master/LICENSE
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pytest-unique.svg
:alt: PyPI
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pytest-unique/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/github/issues-raw/cr3/pytest-unique.svg
:alt: Issues
:target: https://github.com/cr3/pytest-unique/issues
```

## Requirements

You will need the following prerequisites to use pytest-unique:

- Python 3.9, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13

## Installation

To install pytest-unique:

```bash
$ pip install pytest-unique
```

## Usage

You can use the `unique` fixture by passing it as a test argument:

```python
def test_integer(unique):
    assert unique("integer") > 0
```

Here are some of the plugins available by default:

- `bytes` with invalid UTF8 characters.
- `digits` with just numbers.
- `email` with a unique username.
- `float` with a whole part and a decimal part.
- `integer` with `base` and `mod` arguments.
- `password` with `lowercase`, `uppercase`, `digits` and
  `punctuation` arguments.
- `text` with `prefix`, `suffix` and `separator` arguments.

## Extensions

The `unique` fixture can be extended with custom plugins:

```python
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def unique_datetime(unique)
    """Return a datetime unique to this factory instance."""
    timestamp = unique("integer")
    return datetime.fromtimestamp(timestamp, timezone.utc)
```

Then, add it to the `pyproject.toml` file of your project:

```text
[tool.poetry.plugins."pytest_unique"]
datetime = "your_project.unique:unique_datetime"
```

You can then use the plugin like all other plugins:

```python
from datetime import datetime

def test_datetime(unique):
    assert isinstance(unique("datetime"), datetime)
```

## Limitations

The `unique` fixture cannot be used as parameters in parameterized tests
by virtue of being a fixture. The reason it is a fixture rather than a
function is that unique values are generated from an instance of a counter
that returns sequential values. This makes it possible to see the order
in which values were generated which can be useful when troubleshooting.

## Resources

- [Documentation](https://cr3.github.io/pytest-unique/)
- [Release Notes](http://github.com/cr3/pytest-unique/blob/master/CHANGES.rst)
- [Issue Tracker](http://github.com/cr3/pytest-unique/issues)
- [Source Code](http://github.com/cr3/pytest-unique/)
- [PyPi](https://pypi.org/project/pytest-unique/)

documentation: Unique fixture.

functions.name: pytest_unique.fixtures.unique_in_memory
documentation: Memory backed unique.

functions.name: pytest_unique.fixtures.unique_in_file
documentation: File backed unique.

modules.name: pytest_unique.count
documentation:
Counters for iterating over evenly spaced values.

Here is an example of using an in-memory counter:

    >>> counter = memory_count(10, step=2)
    >>> next(counter)
    10
    >>> next(counter)
    12

functions.name: pytest_unique.count.file_count
documentation:
In-file counter.

```
param countfile

Path to count file for persistence.

param start

Optional first count, defaults to 0.

param step

Optional increment between counts, defaults to 1.

raises ValueError

On next() when the countfile doesn't contain a count.
```

functions.name: pytest_unique.count.memory_count
documentation:
In-memory counter.

```
param start

Optional first count, defaults to 0.

param step

Optional increment between counts, defaults to 1.
```

modules.name: pytest_unique.unique
documentation: Unique data generation.

functions.name: pytest_unique.unique.count_factory
documentation: Create a counter that increases with each unique call.

functions.name: pytest_unique.unique.unique_bytes
documentation: Return bytes unique to this factory instance.

functions.name: pytest_unique.unique.unique_digits
documentation:
Return digits unique to this factory instance.

Takes the same arguments as `integer`.

functions.name: pytest_unique.unique.unique_email
documentation:
Return an email unique to this factory instance.

Takes the same arguments as `text`.

functions.name: pytest_unique.unique.unique_float
documentation:
Return a float unique to this factory instance.

The floating point number by making an integer for the whole
part and another integer for the decimal part.

functions.name: pytest_unique.unique.unique_integer
documentation:
Return an integer unique to this factory instance.

```
param base

Optional base to add to the integer.

param mod

Optional modulo to apply on the integer.
```

functions.name: pytest_unique.unique.unique_password
documentation:
Return a password unique to this factory instance.

```
param lowercase

Number of lowercase letters, defaults to 4.

param uppercase

Number of uppercase letters, defaults to 2.

param digits

Number of digits, defaults to 1.

param punctuation

Number of punctuation characters, defaults to 1.
```

functions.name: pytest_unique.unique.unique_text
documentation:
Return text unique to this factory instance.

```
param prefix

Used as a prefix for the unique string, defaults
to a generated string from the stack frame.

param suffix

Optional suffix for the unique string.

param separator

Separator between parts of the string,
defaults to '-'.

param limit

Optional limit for the unique string.
```

functions.name: pytest_unique.unique.unique_uuid
documentation:
Return a UUID unique to this factory instance.

This method provides a more predictable alternative to `uuid4()`.

documentation: Invoke the unique plugin.

methods.name: pytest_unique.unique.Unique.get_plugin
documentation: Find plugin in the registry.

Generate data using plugins.

Plugins are read from the `pytest_unique` entrypoints.

documentation: Entry points based registry management.

functions.name: pytest_unique.registry.get_entry_points
documentation: Get the list of pytest_unique entry points.

functions.name: pytest_unique.registry.registry_load
documentation: Find all installed entry points.

functions.name: pytest_unique.registry.registry_add
documentation:
Add an entry to a registry.

```
param group

Group of the entry.

param name

Name of the entry.

param entry

Entry to add.

param registry

Optional registry to update.

return

A registry with the entry.
```

functions.name: pytest_unique.registry.registry_remove
documentation:
Remove an entry from a registry.

If the entry doesn't exist, return silently.

```
param group

Group of the entry.

param name

Name of the entry.

param registry

Optional registry to update.
```

functions.name: pytest_unique.registry.registry_get
documentation:
Get an entry from a registry.

If the registry is not defined or the group is not in the registry,
the registry is loaded again.

```
param group

Group of the entry.

param name

Name of the entry.

param registry

Optional registry to get from.

raises KeyError

If not found.
```

```

documentation:

If the lock was never acquired or already
released.
```
